extraction.one_line_summary: MUSKAAN Johnny attacked from bottom of summit finish to Mashan scenic point, took solo stage win and race lead.
tactical_patterns: MUSKAAN attacked immediately at climb base rather than waiting for final kilometers, proving early aggression works on steep finishes.
Trek-Segafredo and EF Education First controlled peloton through feedzone despite having GC climbers, preventing early breakaway gap.
Team Sky allowed MUSKAAN complete freedom despite being race favorites, suggesting internal leadership uncertainty or deliberate dual-card strategy.

rider_signals.MUSKAAN Johnny: positive: solo victory from climb base after 'tough controversial season' suggests form peaked perfectly for late-season races.
rider_signals.GROOTEGARTNER Felix: positive: second place on summit finish signals unexpected climbing form for rider not previously flagged as GC threat.
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: negative: dropped by MUSKAAN and failed to podium despite EF's tempo control suggests declining form post-Vuelta.
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: neutral: animated all three intermediate sprints from breakaway but provides useful domestique signal for future breakaway viability testing.
